title: Smythea bombaiensis

Smythea bombaiensis is often classified as a member of the genus Smythea, which is part of the family Vitaceae. The plant is known for its unique characteristics, including its white, star-shaped flowers. Smythea bombaiensis is also classified as a climber or a vine, using its tendrils to support its growth.

In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat fever and other infectious diseases due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
